story.title: Welcome to Pixel Forge: Git Basics
story.narrative: Before you write a single line of game code, you need to understand how we save our work. At Pixel Forge, we don't use 'save as v2'. We use Git. It's our time machine.

story.title: Python: Day One
story.narrative: You've joined Pixel Forge Studio. Sarah, the Lead Dev, hands you your first task: a splash screen script. 'Python is our glue,' she says.

story.title: Decision Trees
story.narrative: Sarah stops by. 'Our NPCs are just standing there. We need logic. If player is close, attack. If health is low, retreat.' You're learning if/else and loops.

story.title: Reusable Logic
story.narrative: 'Stop repeating yourself!' Sarah laughs. 'Write a function for that damage calculation and call it wherever you need.'

story.title: The Inventory System
story.narrative: Marcus needs help. 'We have too many items. We need Lists and Dictionaries to organize this loot.'

story.title: Blueprint for Heroes
story.narrative: 'This codebase is getting huge,' Sarah says. 'Let's use Classes to organize our game entities.'

story.title: The Big Release
story.narrative: It's launch day. You've mastered Python foundations. Now, prove it by shipping your final capstone project.

quiz.question: You are ready to be a Python Dev.

story.title: Lua: The Embedded Engine
story.narrative: Elena slides you a new tablet. 'Python is great for tools, but inside our game engine, we embed Lua. It's ridiculously fast and lightweight. Your first task: print a debug message.'

quiz.question: Which index does a Lua table array start at?

story.title: Scripted Events
story.narrative: 'We need to trigger a trap if the player steps on a specific tile,' Elena explains. 'You'll use if statements to check the player's coordinates.'

story.title: Game Callbacks
story.narrative: 'Stop hardcoding events,' Elena says. 'Define functions for OnPlayerEnter and OnPlayerExit. The C++ engine will call your Lua functions when needed.'

story.title: The Almighty Table
story.narrative: Elena points to the screen. 'In Lua, there are no arrays, lists, or dictionaries. There are only Tables. They do everything.'

quiz.question: How to create an empty table?

story.title: Metatables & OOP
story.narrative: 'Lua doesn't have classes by default,' Elena smiles. 'But we can fake it using Metatables. It's powerful, but you have to wire the plumbing yourself.'

quiz.question: What is self represented by in Lua methods?

story.title: Engine Scripter
story.narrative: You're now an official Engine Scripter. Your Lua scripts are running in production, controlling everything from UI to Boss AI.

quiz.question: Lua is primarily an embedded language.

story.title: The Console Blinks Back
story.narrative: Your first frontend ticket: add a live word count. 'Just JavaScript,' the senior dev says.

story.title: Event Listeners
story.narrative: We need the UI to react to clicks. Use event listeners and if statements to branch logic.

story.title: Arrow Functions
story.narrative: Modern JS uses arrow functions. Let's refactor these clunky callbacks.

story.title: Arrays & Objects
story.narrative: JSON is everywhere. You need to master Object manipulation and Array mapping.

story.title: Classes & Prototypes
story.narrative: Time to build reusable UI components using ES6 Classes.

story.title: Frontend Architect
story.narrative: You are now managing complex state in the browser.

story.title: Go: Scaling the Backend
story.narrative: 'Our matchmaking server needs to handle 50,000 players,' Marcus explains. 'We use Go for that.'

story.title: For Only
story.narrative: Go has no 'while' loop. It only uses 'for'. It's simple and consistent.

story.title: Multiple Returns
story.narrative: Go functions can return multiple values, commonly used for (result, error) patterns.

story.title: Slices & Maps
story.narrative: Arrays are rigid. Slices are flexible. We use them for everything.

story.title: Structs & Interfaces
story.narrative: Go isn't strictly OOP. We use Structs for data and Interfaces for behavior.

story.title: Concurrency Master
story.narrative: Goroutines and Channels are why we use Go. You've tamed the Hydra.

story.title: C# and the Unity Engine
story.narrative: Sarah leads you to the Unity team. 'C# is our primary engine language.'

story.title: Types & Flow
story.narrative: C# is strictly typed. You must explicitly declare variable types and logic bounds.

story.title: Methods
story.narrative: In C#, functions are called Methods and usually live inside a Class.

story.title: Lists & LINQ
story.narrative: We use generic Lists and LINQ to filter thousands of entities quickly.

story.title: OOP Master
story.narrative: Interfaces, Inheritance, and Polymorphism are the pillars of Unity dev.

story.title: Engine Architect
story.narrative: Your C# systems now power the core combat loops.

story.title: C++: The Performance King
story.narrative: 'If you want to move 10,000 entities at 120 FPS, you need C++,' the lead physicist says.

story.title: Tight Loops
story.narrative: We don't waste cycles. Optimize your loops and conditionals.

story.title: Pointers & References
story.narrative: Welcome to memory management. You must master passing by reference vs value.

story.title: The STL
story.narrative: The Standard Template Library gives you std::vector and std::map.

story.title: RAII & Smart Pointers
story.narrative: No more memory leaks. Use std::unique_ptr to manage object lifetimes.

quiz.question: Which pointer automatically deletes its object?

story.title: Metal Worker
story.narrative: You are writing the core engine code that makes our games blazing fast.

story.title: Rust: Memory Safety
story.narrative: 'We're rewriting our netcode in Rust,' Sarah says. 'It's strict, but safe.'

story.title: Pattern Matching
story.narrative: The 'match' keyword is incredibly powerful. Use it to exhaustively handle control flow.

story.title: Ownership & Borrowing
story.narrative: Welcome to the Borrow Checker. It will frustrate you, then save you from memory leaks.

story.title: Vectors & HashMaps
story.narrative: Using dynamic collections safely requires understanding Lifetimes.

story.title: Traits & Structs
story.narrative: Rust doesn't have classes. We define data in Structs and behavior via Traits.

story.title: Fearless Concurrency
story.narrative: You've proven you can write safe, concurrent Rust code. The compiler loves you.

quiz.question: Rust prevents data races at compile time.

labs.title: Splash Screen
labs.starter_code: print('--- Pixel Forge ---')
labs.solution:
print('--- Pixel Forge ---')
print('Ready.')

labs.title: Combat Check
labs.starter_code:
hp = 10
# TODO: if hp < 5 print 'Low'
labs.solution: if hp < 5: print('Low')

labs.title: Damage Function
labs.starter_code:
def deal_damage(hp, dmg):
    pass
labs.solution: def deal_damage(hp, dmg): return hp - dmg

labs.title: Loot List
labs.starter_code:
loot = ['Gold']
# TODO: append 'Gem'
labs.solution:
loot = ['Gold', 'Gem']
print(', '.join(loot))

labs.title: Hero Class
labs.starter_code:
class Hero:
    def __init__(self, n): self.name = n
labs.solution:
h = Hero('Alex')
print(h.name)

labs.title: Debug Log
labs.starter_code: -- Print 'Engine Started'
labs.solution: print('Engine Started')

labs.title: Health Check
labs.starter_code:
hp = 10
-- if hp < 5 print 'Heal'
labs.solution: if hp < 5 then print('Heal') end

labs.title: Math Func
labs.starter_code:
function add(a, b)
end
labs.solution: function add(a, b) return a + b end

labs.title: Item Table
labs.starter_code:
items = {'Sword', 'Shield'}
-- print the first item
labs.solution:
items = {'Sword', 'Shield'}
print(items[1])

labs.title: Metatable Magic
Console Message
labs.starter_code: console.log('Test');
labs.solution: console.log('Test');

labs.title: Even or Odd
labs.starter_code: let n = 4; // print 'even'
labs.solution: let n = 4; if (n % 2 === 0) console.log('even');

labs.title: Multiplier Func
labs.starter_code: const mul = (a, b) => a * b;
labs.solution: const mul = (a, b) => a * b;

labs.title: Object Keys
labs.starter_code: let obj = {x: 10}; console.log(obj.x);
labs.solution: let obj = {x: 10}; console.log(obj.x);

labs.title: UI Class
labs.starter_code: class UI { constructor() { this.name = 'btn'; } }
labs.solution: class UI { constructor() { this.name = 'btn'; } }

labs.title: Go Fmt
For Loop
Multiple Returns
Slice
Struct
Console Write
labs.starter_code: System.Console.WriteLine("C#");
labs.solution: System.Console.WriteLine("C#");

labs.title: If Bounds
labs.starter_code: int x=5; if(x>0) System.Console.WriteLine("Pos");
labs.solution: int x=5; if(x>0) System.Console.WriteLine("Pos");

labs.title: Void Method
labs.starter_code: class P { static void Msg(){ System.Console.WriteLine("M"); } static void Main(){ Msg(); } }
labs.solution: class P { static void Msg(){ System.Console.WriteLine("M"); } static void Main(){ Msg(); } }

labs.title: Array Length
labs.starter_code: class P { static void Main(){ int[] a = {1}; System.Console.WriteLine(a.Length); } }
labs.solution: class P { static void Main(){ int[] a = {1}; System.Console.WriteLine(a.Length); } }

labs.title: Class Instance
labs.starter_code: class E{ public int hp=10; } class P{ static void Main(){ E e = new E(); System.Console.WriteLine(e.hp); } }
labs.solution: class E{ public int hp=10; } class P{ static void Main(){ E e = new E(); System.Console.WriteLine(e.hp); } }

labs.title: C++ Out
labs.starter_code:
#include <iostream>
int main() { std::cout << "C++"; return 0; }
labs.solution:
#include <iostream>
int main() { std::cout << "C++"; return 0; }

labs.title: Switch
labs.starter_code:
#include <iostream>
int main() { int x=1; switch(x){ case 1: std::cout<<"One"; } return 0; }
labs.solution:
#include <iostream>
int main() { int x=1; switch(x){ case 1: std::cout<<"One"; } return 0; }

labs.title: Pointer
labs.starter_code:
#include <iostream>
int main() { int x=5; int* p = &x; std::cout<<*p; return 0; }
labs.solution:
#include <iostream>
int main() { int x=5; int* p = &x; std::cout<<*p; return 0; }

labs.title: Vector
Class
labs.starter_code:
#include <iostream>
class A { public: int x = 3; }; int main() { A a; std::cout<<a.x; return 0; }
labs.solution:
#include <iostream>
class A { public: int x = 3; }; int main() { A a; std::cout<<a.x; return 0; }

labs.title: Mut
labs.starter_code: fn main() { let mut x = 1; x = 2; println!("{}", x); }
labs.solution: fn main() { let mut x = 1; x = 2; println!("{}", x); }

labs.title: Match
labs.starter_code: fn main() { let x=1; match x { 1 => println!("1"), _ => () } }
labs.solution: fn main() { let x=1; match x { 1 => println!("1"), _ => () } }

labs.title: Borrow
labs.starter_code:
fn p(s: &String){println!("{}", s);}
fn main(){let s=String::from("R"); p(&s);}
labs.solution:
fn p(s: &String){println!("{}", s);}
fn main(){let s=String::from("R"); p(&s);}

labs.title: Vec
labs.starter_code: fn main(){let v = vec![5]; println!("{}", v[0]);}
labs.solution: fn main(){let v = vec![5]; println!("{}", v[0]);}

labs.title: Struct
labs.starter_code: struct P{x:i32} fn main(){let p=P{x:8}; println!("{}", p.x);}
labs.solution: struct P{x:i32} fn main(){let p=P{x:8}; println!("{}", p.x);}

projects.title: Forge Greeting
projects.briefing: Sarah wants a script that greets new developers and collects their department info.

phases.title: Basic Inputs
phases.solution:
name = input('Name: ')
dept = input('Dept: ')
print(f'Welcome {name} to {dept}!')

projects.title: Loot Tracker Pro
projects.description: Manage game inventory items with quantities.
projects.briefing: Track loot drops using dictionaries to store item names and counts.

projects.title: Roguelike Quest
projects.briefing: Sarah needs a combat engine prototype with classes and random encounters.

projects.title: Save Cryptographer
projects.description: Secure game save files using encryption algorithms.
projects.briefing: Protect player data. Implement a custom XOR cipher and Base64 encoding for save files.

projects.title: Build Master
projects.description: Distributed task runner for studio build automation.
projects.briefing: Architect a system that distributes build tasks to worker nodes via sockets.

projects.title: PyVM
projects.description: Stack-based Virtual Machine for a custom bytecode language.
projects.briefing: Touch the metal. Build a VM that parses and executes a custom instruction set (PUSH, ADD, PRINT).

projects.title: NeuroNPC
projects.description: Neural Network implementation for evolving NPC behavior.
projects.briefing: Create a backpropagation neural network from scratch (no external libs) to train NPCs.

phases.title: The Neuron
phases.starter_code:
import math
def sigmoid(x): return 1 / (1 + math.exp(-x))

projects.title: Config Loader
projects.briefing: Load game settings from a Lua table.

projects.title: NPC Dialog
projects.briefing: Use nested tables for dialog options.

projects.title: UI Builder
projects.briefing: Build a flex-box like system in Lua.

projects.title: Console Log
projects.briefing: Log studio tasks to C# console.

projects.title: Memory Allocator
projects.briefing: Manage memory at the byte level.
